question: Why should decoupling capacitors be placed close to the IC power pins?
options: A
Helps delay power on time to avoid interference suddenly
B
Facilitate the replacement or removal of capacitor parts during the production test phase
C
It can effectively filter out power supply noise and provide instantaneous current response
D
Provide a uniform heat dissipation path to avoid overheating of the power supply IC
E
None of the above options are correct

question: What should be considered when placing the power input port?

It should be as close as possible to the main control chip and the ADC module
B
Close to the edge of the board and reserve enough copper skin for current bearing
C
It should be arranged in the middle of the board to facilitate and balance the routing in all directions
D
It should be close to the analog area to reduce the power supply delay path
E
None of the above options are correct

None of the above options are correct
